text.title: pm-certification-changes-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for pm-certification-changes-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: The README is a single-page summary; it omits the actual ECO (Examination Content Outline) tasks/enablers per domain. Agents that quote percentages without ECO tasks produce shallow study plans.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchor to PMI Standard for Earned Value (2024), PMBOK 7e, Agile Practice Guide, and the published 2026 ECO PDF.

failure-mode.description: "Sustainability" and "Tailoring" are PMI buzzwords; LLMs hallucinate plausible-sounding but non-PMI definitions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchor content to authoritative PMI sources: PMBOK 7e, Agile Practice Guide, Process Groups Practice Guide.

failure-mode.description: Domain weights are not exam question counts; PMI uses scaled scoring per domain. Agents that promise "you must get 26% of Business Environment correct" are wrong.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PMI scaled scoring is not public. Use weights to allocate study effort, not to predict raw question distributions.

failure-mode.description: Exam item bank evolves continuously after July 2026; static content goes stale within months of release.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plan for ongoing revision. Treat curriculum as a living document, not a one-time PDF.

failure-mode.description: Regional language exams (UA, RU, ES, ZH) lag English by 1–3 quarters; weights are the same but examples differ.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Translate examples with PMI vocabulary, not literal translation. Maintain language parity.

failure-mode.description: Generating PMP mock questions with LLMs without human PMP-holder review before delivery.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs invent plausible PMI vocabulary that does not appear in the ECO or source texts. Auto-generated PMP items distributed to candidates without review damage exam credibility and candidate preparation.

failure-mode.description: Treating domain weights as direct question-count percentages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PMI uses scaled scoring per domain; "26% Business Environment" does not mean 26% of raw questions map to that domain directly. Agents that promise specific question counts mislead candidates.

failure-mode.description: Applying the 2026 ECO to candidates sitting before July 1, 2026.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pre-cutover candidates are tested on the 2021 outline; using the new weights for them introduces wrong material and creates confusion about what to prioritize.

failure-mode.description: LLMs invent PMI vocabulary that "sounds right" (e.g., "Strategic Tailoring Index").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a glossary check against PMBOK 7e index before accepting any term.

failure-mode.description: Domain weight drift: agents fed old outlines (42/50/8) will silently regress.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the 33/41/26 weights in the system prompt, not a retrieval doc that can be evicted.

failure-mode.description: "Business Environment" is the largest growth area and most under-represented in legacy material — quotas alone do not fix shallow content; require new examples per enabler, not paraphrases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invest in original content creation, not repackaging.

failure-mode.description: Distractor design is hard for LLMs — they often produce options that are obviously wrong or two-correct.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run a self-critique pass on every generated question.

failure-mode.description: Region/language: same weights, different idioms. Translation agents that copy English idioms into UA/PT lose realism.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Employ bilingual PMI-certified reviewers for regional variants.

failure-mode.description: Citation hallucination: LLMs love fake page numbers. Validate citations by retrieving the cited PDF page text and checking for keyword overlap before publishing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Build a validation hook into the publishing pipeline.

failure-mode.description: Never let an agent decide if a candidate is "ready"; emit readiness signals (sub-domain confidence, mock score trend) and let a human instructor make the call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Preserve human judgment on certification decisions.

failure-mode.description: PDU/CCRS reporting: do not auto-submit PDUs on a candidate's behalf — PMI compliance issue; agents only draft.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Maintain audit trail and compliance responsibility with human instructors.
